summary: Server::getLatency() should return null if unset
description:
Since load balancers do not utilize SDAM, RTT will never be calculated. The return value from {{mongoc_server_description_round_trip_time}} will be {{-1}}, which indicates "unset" (see: CDRIVER-4176).

Returning {{null}} in this case may be the most sensible API, but if there is a BC concern we could always return {{-1}} instead. If we do change this method to return {{null}} for an unset value, we should also update {{php_phongo_server_to_zval}} (called by {{php_phongo_server_get_debug_info}}) and ensure the "round_trip_time" field is reported consistently.

{{Server::getLatency()}} is currently only tested for replica sets ([tests/replicaset/server-001.phpt|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/master/tests/replicaset/server-001.phpt]), so we should consider adding more test coverage when addressing this issue.

summary: CommandSucceededEvent debug info omits fields
description: This issue dates back to [2f88011|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/2f8801145039382dd31545115f739b6201051140#diff-4f94e6d9f39f7bb7dfcf7f1749b2ca9a2839ae571403977204d0b25a68a2e913R224] for PHPC-348.

summary: Incorrect return type in Server::getLatency() documentation
description: [Server::getLatency()|https://www.php.net/manual/en/mongodb-driver-server.getlatency.php] returns an integer, not a string.

summary: ReadPreference for Manager::selectServer() should be optional and default to primary
description:
If the read preference argument for [{{mongoc_client_select_server}}|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/1.18.0/mongoc_client_select_server.html] is null, libmongoc will attempt select a primary by default. There is no reason that {{Manager::selectServer()}} cannot do the same.

As an added benefit, this would simplify many uses of this method within PHPC and PHPLIB, which commonly construct a primary read preference.

summary: Bump libmongoc requirement in config.m4 to 1.19.1
description:
This was missed when bumping to libmongoc 1.19-dev in [ac49353|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/ac4935338a65d7f3370befd5c245e1be68f9eeba] for PHPC-1761.

Additionally, it looks like this was not caught earlier due to the build axis for custom libmongoc versions not working as intended (PHPC-1994).

summary: Explicitly implement Stringable for PHP 8+
description: Stringable was introduced in PHP 8.0. From PHP 8.1, it will be implicitly implemented for classes that have a {{__toString()}} method; however, explicit implementation is still encouraged. We should do so when compiling for PHP 8.0+.

summary: Support BSON Binary subtype 0x07 (column)
Type validation for autoEncryption.extraOptions
description:
{{autoEncryption.extraOptions}} should be validated as an array or object. If a different type is provided, the option appears to be silently ignored and an empty document is assigned as the extra options.

This was likely missed in [mongodb/mongo-php-driver@b8c8523|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/b8c8523e1c4d85a560c99f3ca0343c86fe283d2c] for PHPC-1545.

summary: Specify string return type in __toString() arginfos
Defer to libmongoc for cross-option URI validation
description:
CDRIVER-3723 will introduce a {{mongoc_client_new_from_uri_with_error}} method that will take a {{mongoc_uri_t}} and validate it before constructing a client. This can then replace our usage of {{mongoc_client_new_from_uri}}, which has no error reporting. URI options applied from SRV records will also be validated, which will allow exceptions to be reported upon Manager construction instead of the first instance of server selection. 

Additionally, it will allow us to delete our own "finalize" functions, which were copied from libmongoc's private API:

 * {{php_phongo_uri_finalize_auth}}, introduced in [2f94d95|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/2f94d957ca3bbd5d873a8305f118c2fe6c56db53] for PHPC-1346
 * {{php_phongo_uri_finalize_tls}}, introduced [ef16f97|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/ef16f97f6c632b0b7b0e4d68c3b7188a5175b5f9] for PHPC-1288

summary: Lift restriction on authSource without credentials when set in URI options array
description: Similar to PHPC-1538, but that issue only addresses validation through the connection string alone. Relaxing restrictions for {{authSource}} set via the options array will depend on CDRIVER-3723.

summary: Validate loadBalanced URI array option
description:
When load balancer support was introduced in PHPC-1752, we forgot to add specific validation when {{loadBalanced}} is specified in the URI options array.

Additionally, we should add tests for validation in the connection string (handled entirely by libmongoc) and URI options array (to be implemented by this ticket).

summary: ServerDescription::getType() should return a string
description: Change ServerDescription::getType() to return a string and define string values for those constants (they are currently defined as integer values). This will be more convenient for users, since the constants' values will be meaningful on their own. This should be done similarly to the ReadPreference constants ([https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.mongodb-driver-readpreference.php]).

summary: Implement Server::getServerDescription
description: This can help with testing the ServerDescription object introduced in PHPC-1805.

summary: Check for uninitialized intern in ServerDescription get_properties_hash
description:
php_phongo_serverdescription_get_properties_hash() should return early if intern is uninitialized. After the call to 
PHONGO_GET_PROPERTY_HASH_INIT_PROPS, there should be a check that intern->server_description is a non-zero pointer. See https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/1244#discussion_r683046524

summary: Support load balancer in ServerDescription and TopologyDescription
Warn if ServerDescription::getLastUpdateTime() must be truncated
description: As is done for WriteConcern's {{wTimeoutMS}} field (PHPC-1411), we should raise a warning if {{ServerDescription::getLastUpdateTime()}} truncates a 64-bit value on a 32-bit platform. Additionally, the field can be reported as a string instead of an integer in ServerDescriptions debug info (i.e. {{var_dump()}} and {{var_export()}}).

summary: Allow observation of commands issued during mongoc_client_destroy()
description:
In order to allow observation of ServerClosedEvent and TopologyClosedEvent (PHPC-2023), {{php_phongo_pclient_destroy}} no longer clears APM callbacks unless PHP is already in request shutdown. This allows those SDAM events to be observed for request-scoped clients; however, they still cannot be observed for persistent clients since those are freed during GSHUTDOWN.

With that change, it will now be possible to observe command monitoring events for commands issued during {{mongoc_client_destroy()}} (e.g. {{endSessions}}) for request-scoped clients. This will entail swapping the order in which Managers and clients are unregistered in {{php_phongo_manager_free_object}}.

summary: Expose whether a client session is dirty
description:
CDRIVER-3780 introduced {{mongoc_client_session_get_dirty}}. Implementation of various session spec tests (PHPLIB-528) require that we expose this information through PHPC.

We can consider either introducing a public API (e.g. {{isDirty()}} method) or expose this through debug info (assuming PHPLIB can infer from that).

summary: Report more concise debug info for ended sessions
description:
{{php_phongo_session_get_debug_info}} currently branches for every field and reports {{NULL}} if the session has ended (i.e. internal {{client_session}} pointer is null).

We should consider just changing the reported debug info for sessions that have been ended. For example, we might omit all of these fields and report an "ended" field as true.

summary: RHEL 7.0 and 7.1 builds fail due to incompatible pointer warnings
description:
RHEL 7.0 and 7.1 builds are failing due to incompatible pointer warnings, which are reported as errors thanks to {{mongodb-developer-flags}} using {{-Werror}}. We may be able to temporarily work around this by disabling developer flags for those builds; otherwise, we'll need to wait for an upstream fix (CDRIVER-4237).

Interestingly, this was only introduced when upgrading to libmongoc 1.20.0 (PHPC-2011) and not the earlier bump to 1.20-dev for the new atomic APIs (PHPC-1997).

summary: Memory leak in php_phongo_zval_to_bson_value
description:
The original implementation of {{php_phongo_zval_to_bson_value}} from [f167da9|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/f167da97d46f2f0a2f27a41c0b01289732b95c58#diff-e44ba6debc260acae4810523ccc8a38a02d6db3fefa2bd4f88514f6ff1383ed7R654] for PHPC-1499 does not free the {{bson_t}} used to hold the intermediary result between zval conversion and {{bson_value_t}} extraction.

This function was previously only used by {{ClientEncryption::encrypt()}} and {{decrypt()}}. The leak was discovered when implementing supports for the BulkWrite and Query {{comment}} options in PHPC-2049.

summary: Various bson_value_t memory leaks in ClientEncryption methods
description:
The {{bson_value_t* keyid}} parameter to {{mongoc_client_encryption_create_datakey}} is always initialized and must be freed. This dates back to [e65a933|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/e65a933c8f8ab9bc2729c46c3d3c39d6414fab40] for PHPC-1499.

Additionally, the {{ClientEncryption::encrypt()}} and {{decrypt()}} methods have several leaks where {{bson_value_t}} structs are not freed (distinct from PHPC-2088). Those leaks date back to [4d7b35e|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/4d7b35ee69c9fb07d1af74fd4cceb95f14a20f60] and [f0372a5|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/f0372a50c5f00b964b3d959c9c0d929d1733de58].

summary: Relay command comment when constructing a cursor
description: When a {{MongoDB\Driver\Command}} is created with a {{comment}} option in the command document, that option must be relayed to to {{mongoc_cursor_new_from_command_reply_with_opts}} from within {{phongo_execute_command}} (as is currently done for {{cursor.batchSize}}). This ensures that libmongoc can attach the {{comment}} to subsequent {{getMore}} operations for commands that yield a cursor (e.g. aggregate, listCollections).

summary: phongo_clientencryption_init uses wrong signature when compiling without CSFLE
description:
[d8feba4|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/d8feba4bd121662c31fa8960f6741d9032b0089f] for PHPC-2038 neglected to update the signature of {{phongo_clientencryption_init}} when the extension is compiled without CSFLE support.

This is not a build error, as the {{ClientEncryption.h}} header is not included from {{ClientEncryption.c}} (only {{Manager.c}}), so it need not be fixed in the 1.13.x branch.

summary: Bump maxWireVersion for MongoDB 6.0
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2314}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: EncryptOpts queryType should accept a string consistent with queryType in encryptedFields
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2352}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Add return type information to final classes
description:
Since most of the classes in the {{MongoDB}} namespace, we can start adding return type information to the zend_arginfo struct. This can be done by replacing {{ZEND_BEGIN_ARG_INFO}} with either {{ZEND_BEGIN_ARG_WITH_RETURN_OBJ_INFO}} (PHP 7.4+) or {{ZEND_BEGIN_ARG_WITH_RETURN_TYPE_INFO}} (PHP 7.0+) as appropriate.

On PHP 8 and later, we can also make use of union types. This should be done conditionally when compiling for PHP 8.

summary: Add return type information to interfaces
description: Similar to PHPC-1708, except for interfaces instead of final classes. This is a BC break and can only be done in 2.0, and only after the change has been communicated to users (see PHPC-1710).

summary: Use stub files to generate arginfo
description: This technique was introduced in [php/php-src#4284|https://github.com/php/php-src/pull/4284] and discussed in [this internals thread|https://externals.io/message/105970]. According to [this post|https://externals.io/message/116308#116610], stubs can even be used to generate arginfo for older PHP versions (e.g. 5.x).

summary: Upgrade libmongocrypt dependency to 1.5.2
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2403}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Reduce expected removeKeyAltName operations to a single findOneAndUpdate
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2356}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Allow RewrapManyDataKeyResult.bulkWriteResult to be optional
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2367}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Ensure CRYPT_SHARED_LIB_PATH is exported for Evergreen tests
description:
PHPC-2099 supposedly added crypt_shared testing to the Evergreen CI matrix by way of a {{CRYPT_SHARED_LIB_PATH}}; however, it failed to properly export that so it could be read as via {{getenv()}} in the PHPC test suite. As a result, {{crypt_shared}} is not properly being tested (see: [task log|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_php_driver_test_csfle_crypt_shared__mongodb_versions~6.0_php_edge_versions~latest_stable_os~debian11_test_crypt_shared_2b03e66bbb0e9933e8d280de908f89cc4c4ca1ea_22_08_26_09_37_40/0?type=T#L1301]).

Note: there is no related issue for PHPLIB as it has always exported the variable (see: [mongodb/mongo-php-library@265810a|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/265810afc9c19247fded2d5530793ef1a91bf340#diff-2bc841e86ce96b7b422ae203fd8315d0b2a461956cecbe0e096420656fc3fb12R249] for PHPLIB-891).

summary: Remove unnecessary casts to zend_long
description: php_phongo.c and Server.c contain unnecessary casts to zend_long for the server description round trip time. See [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/1239#discussion_r679277511]

summary: Add final modifier to __set_state methods
description: The method declarations for {{__set_state}} omits {{ZEND_ACC_FINAL}}. This appears to be an oversight, as most other class methods are final (as are the classes themselves).

summary: Default Binary constructor $type parameter to TYPE_GENERIC
description: See: https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.mongodb-bson-binary.php#mongodb-bson-binary.constants.type-generic

summary: Allow applications to detect crypt_shared availability
description:
Related to: [Detecting crypt_shared Availability|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/client-side-encryption/client-side-encryption.rst#detecting-crypt_shared-availability] in the CSFLE spec.

This depends on libmongoc providing some API to do so (see: CDRIVER-4414).

Since only one instance of the {{crypt_shared}} library should be loaded at any given time, it should be possible to report this via {{phpinfo()}} (as we do for libmongoc and libmongocrypt versions) by querying the internal client or Manager registry. If we're concerned about objects being removed from the registry (e.g. Manager using a non-persistent client), we could implement some sort of caching to check for {{crypt_shared}} after constructing a Manager and recording that in some internal global for the duration of the process.

summary: get_module_info() test function may fail to match some phpinfo() values
description:
The {{get_module_info()}} helper for tests dates back to [f4cf0fe|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/f4cf0fe3a0ed8ed5db6e3c022abba178011f4feb#diff-00f2ff26ea77cd8bb336eba576b7bf2506e2312f6807fd83810ded0950553a32R22] for PHPC-1113.

The regex pattern used to match {{phpinfo()}} values only matches word characters (i.e. letters, numbers, and underscores). This can be problematic for values that contain version strings, among other things.

This ticket came up while working on PHPC-2103, and has a corresponding PHPLIB ticket since this old code was recently lifted to that project.

summary: WriteResult getters should return null and emit deprecation notice for w:0 writes and return zero otherwise if libmongoc result is omitted
description:
[{{MongoDB\Driver\WriteResult}}|https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.mongodb-driver-writeresult.php] getters currently return null if the libmongoc result document omits an expected field. 


 * Continue to return {{null}} for unacknowledged writes, but raise a deprecation notice informing users that this may change in 2.0 (see: PHPC-2144)
* Return 0 for any acknowledged writes if the field is not present. This may not actually happen in libmongoc, but it'd be a good defensive approach since results are BSON documents.

summary: Validate masterKey option for createDataKey() and rewrapManyDataKey()
description: Validate the expected type of the "masterKey" option before invoking {{php_phongo_zval_to_bson}}, since it expects to receive an array or object.

summary: ClientEncryption::createDataKey() may leak when processing masterKey option
description: {{ClientEncryption::createDataKey()}} uses a stack-allocated {{bson_t}} for processing the "masterKey" option. This is never freed, which could lead to a leak if the document ever exceeds the internal limit and triggers dynamic allocation.

summary: Error if RewrapManyDataKey is called with masterKey and without provider
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2441}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: CheckResolv.m4 fails to link libresolv when detecting res_nsearch
Consider enums and traits in BSON decoding instantiatable checks
description:
[PHONGO_IS_CLASS_INSTANTIATABLE|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.14.2/src/phongo_bson.c#L31] is checks {{zend_class_entry}} flags and is used for two purposes:

 * When encountering a {{\_\_pclass}} Binary field in a document, only an instantiatable class can be used as the ODM class; otherwise, it is ignored and the {{\_\_pclass}} field will show up in the deserialized PHP document.
 * When specifying a class in a type map, it must be instantiatable or else an exception is raised.

I came across this when implementing PHPC-2083 as I realized the macro did not consider enums nor traits. We should add both along with regression tests for all class types presently supported (e.g. abstract, interfaces).

summary: Use consistent int types for APM fields and snprintf for string conversions
description:
Several things I noticed dating back to the original implementation in PHPC-349:

 * Structs use {{uint64_t}} for fields where libmongoc's API returns {{int64_t}}. It's unlikely that these values would ever be negative, but we should keep the original type.
 * Request and operation ID are printed as unsigned strings. If a negative value was returned by libmongoc for some reason, this would alter it.
 * Printing request and operation ID uses {{sprintf}}. Although it's unlikely we'd overflow the string buffer, it'd be safer to use {{snprintf}} as we do in the {{ZVAL_INT64_STRING}} macro.

I came across this while working on PHPC-2143.

summary: Expose server error replies in BulkWriteResult
description: CDRIVER-4425 may introduce an {{errorReplies}} array on {{mongoc_write_result_t}}, which collects raw server responses from failed write commands within a bulk write operation. If BulkWriteResult provides access to this field, PHPLIB will then be able to run _all_ spec tests associated with DRIVERS-2385 (removing skips in [2e99cd8|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/commit/2e99cd83f551cd28d022c7e80dbd47ed4c558dcc#diff-58846c56a14f8262956f22c9563867bcdc800ce8159e1037b480ad5b63889b72R68] for PHPLIB-909 ).

summary: Write concern error without message results in null pointer access
description: While adding the test described in PHPLIB-935, I discovered that a write concern error without an error message results in a null pointer access in the {{WriteConcernError}} class. As unlikely as the error is to appear in the wild, we should still guard against this.

summary: Build Windows DLLs for PHP 7.4
description: Windows DLLs are built for all supported PHP versions, except for PHP 7.4, see https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/1380#issuecomment-1377190901

summary: Permit tlsDisableOCSPEndpointCheck in KMS TLS options
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2507}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.
